title: Flaming Moes

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's iconic frustrated exclamation opening the episode
jokes.punchline: The signature catchphrase delivery
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, though predictable

jokes.text: Wow. Info-tainment.
jokes.setup: Kent Brockman introducing Eye on Springfield
jokes.punchline: Homer's portmanteau coining the term 'info-tainment'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that was prescient - info-tainment became a real media term

jokes.text: 25 years and still burning strong
jokes.setup: Describing a tire fire as if it's an achievement
jokes.punchline: Treating an environmental disaster as something to celebrate
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - celebrating a perpetual disaster as a milestone

jokes.text: He's not so fat.
jokes.punchline: Homer's defensive reaction about his own weight
jokes.explanation: Character-specific humor - Homer's predictable defensiveness about weight

jokes.text: It was a dump. If you see me there, you know I up bad.
jokes.punchline: Blunt dismissal of his hometown on TV
jokes.explanation: Good character voice - the boxer's unfiltered honesty about his origins

jokes.text: Whoa-- T and A!
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude reduction to sexual terms
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer crudeness - character-appropriate but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Who can sleep with those five evil hens cackling and plotting against me?
jokes.punchline: Bart's paranoid characterization of the girls as scheming enemies
jokes.explanation: Excellent character voice - Bart's dramatic paranoia about little girls

jokes.text: They've got better things to talk about than you. Sheesh, what an ego.
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about girls plotting against him
jokes.punchline: Marge deflating his self-importance
jokes.explanation: Good maternal deflation of Bart's ego - solid setup/punchline structure

jokes.text: Lisa, your brother is so gross. You should smell his room.
jokes.setup: Immediately after Marge says they have better things to talk about
jokes.punchline: The girls immediately start mocking Bart
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing - proves Bart right immediately after Marge dismisses him

jokes.text: It's a mop. My husband will be a janitor.
jokes.setup: Wax fortune telling at slumber party
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact acceptance of lowly prediction
jokes.explanation: Simple setup-punchline - the deadpan resignation to a grim future

jokes.text: That looks like an Olympic torch to me. He could be an athlete and go on to have a great acting career.
jokes.setup: Same wax reading being interpreted differently
jokes.punchline: Overly optimistic spin on the same shape
jokes.explanation: Good contrast joke - same object, wildly different interpretations

jokes.text: It's a dustpan. The wax never lies.
jokes.setup: Multiple interpretations of the wax shape
jokes.punchline: Third interpretation delivered with mystical authority
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with the mystical 'wax never lies' - treating silly game seriously

jokes.text: I dare you to... kiss Lisa's brother. Eww!!
jokes.punchline: Universal disgust at kissing Bart
jokes.explanation: Standard kid comedy - predictable but character-appropriate disgust

jokes.text: Who? Mom and dad. Jinx! #You can't talk till somebody says your name #
jokes.punchline: Lisa trapping him with childhood jinx rule
jokes.explanation: Smart sibling warfare - Lisa using kid rules to neutralize Bart's threat

jokes.text: An elephant who never forgets... to brush!
jokes.punchline: Dental hygiene twist
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection joke - subverts the expected 'never forgets' phrase

jokes.text: Because I'm jinxed, damn it!... Ow!
jokes.setup: Bart unable to speak due to jinx
jokes.punchline: Frustrated outburst followed by consequence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Bart's frustration overriding the rule, then getting punished

jokes.text: You spoke while you were jinxed so I get to punch you. Sorry, it's the law.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's mock-serious enforcement of kid rules
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa - treating arbitrary kid rules like serious legal code

jokes.text: People are healthier and drinking less. If it wasn't for the junior high school next door no one would even use the cigarette machine.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of selling cigarettes to minors
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - Moe casually admitting to illegal activity as business concern

jokes.text: Increased job satisfaction and family togetherness are poison for a purveyor of mind-numbing intoxicants.
jokes.punchline: Homer's unexpectedly articulate analysis of the bar business
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character subversion - Homer suddenly speaking like a business analyst

jokes.text: You're too late, Homer. Barney sucked it dry. Cut his gums up pretty bad.
jokes.punchline: Barney literally sucking the tap dry to the point of injury
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy - Barney's desperate alcoholism taken to absurd extreme

jokes.text: Gin and... tonic? Do they mix?
jokes.punchline: Not knowing if basic cocktail ingredients mix
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe incompetence - claiming expertise while being utterly clueless

jokes.text: And this is Patty trying to plug her razor into those ungodly Czechoslovakian outlets. As you can see, we never got the hang of it.
jokes.punchline: Mundane travel frustration treated as significant
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - the sisters' boring vacation made even more tedious

jokes.text: As I stared at that hairy drumstick I knew I needed a drink.
jokes.punchline: The image driving Homer to drink
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor - vivid imagery justifying Homer's need to drink

jokes.text: If you're going for a beer, this is the last one. D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to escape for a drink
jokes.punchline: Patty blocking his only escape route
jokes.explanation: Good frustration comedy - Patty unknowingly torturing Homer further

jokes.text: It passed the first test-- I didn't go blind.
jokes.punchline: Very low bar for drink safety
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about bootleg alcohol - Homer's hilariously low safety standards

jokes.text: They say everyone can float in the Dead Sea but Selma sank right to the bottom.
jokes.setup: Scientific fact about Dead Sea buoyancy
jokes.punchline: Selma somehow defying physics through her awfulness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup-punchline structure - using scientific fact for character assassination

jokes.text: I don't know the scientific explanation but fire made it good.
jokes.punchline: Homer's simple-minded approach to mixology
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - treating accidental discovery like intentional innovation

jokes.text: Bowie knife, troll doll... glass eye... Oh, here we are.
jokes.setup: Moe looking through lost and found for cough syrup
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bizarre items culminating in finding medicine
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity - the random items in Moe's lost and found box

jokes.text: Homer, it's like there's a party in my mouth and everyone's invited.
jokes.punchline: Overwrought poetic description from the town drunk
jokes.explanation: Great character voice - Barney's unexpectedly eloquent food/drink description

jokes.text: Hey, this drink is delicious and my phlegm feels looser.
jokes.punchline: Disgusting medical side effect mentioned casually
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor - mixing praise with TMI medical information

jokes.text: It's called a Flaming... ...Moe! It's called a Flaming Moe. That's right, a Flaming Moe. My name is Moe, and I invented it.
jokes.punchline: Moe stealing credit and improvising the name change
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Moe's quick thinking and shameless theft in real time

jokes.text: Hey, what's this? A sneeze guard. Wow, it really works!
jokes.punchline: Immediate practical demonstration of sneeze guard effectiveness
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag - immediate cause and effect with sneeze guard

jokes.text: It hasn't been this crowded since the government cracked down on you for accepting food stamps.
jokes.punchline: Reference to Moe's previous illegal activities
jokes.explanation: Good callback humor - establishes Moe's history of shady business practices

jokes.text: Think my drink had something to do with it? Who can say? It's probably a combination of things.
jokes.setup: Obviously the drink caused the crowd
jokes.punchline: Moe's false modesty about obvious cause
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor - Moe's transparent false modesty

jokes.text: You mean, '' Bartenders Do It 'Til You Barf''? Above that store-bought drollery.
jokes.punchline: Dismissing crude bar humor as 'store-bought drollery'
jokes.explanation: Great character voice - sophisticated waitress dismissing bar's crude humor

jokes.text: You could, but I find this way is much more accurate, and fun.
jokes.punchline: Moe's creepy justification for inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate creepiness - Moe being inappropriately handsy with employee

jokes.text: An unforgettable weekend at Club Moe. I'd prefer to vacation someplace hot. - Whoo! - Ha-ha! - Ow! - Whoo!
jokes.punchline: Immediate demonstration that his place is literally hot/on fire
jokes.explanation: Good visual timing - waitress gets her wish for 'hot' vacation immediately

jokes.text: I like your moxie, kid. You're hired. You shan't regret this. Methinks I shan't.
jokes.punchline: Shakespearean language in dive bar context
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - educated waitress and crude Moe speaking alike

jokes.text: Tipsy McStagger is just a composite of other successful logos.
jokes.punchline: Honest admission that mascot is completely artificial
jokes.explanation: Smart corporate satire - admitting mascots are focus-grouped composites

jokes.text: You tell him for me that he makes one great mozzarella stick.
jokes.punchline: Moe still wanting to give personal compliment to fake character
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Moe missing the point about fake mascots

jokes.text: Do you know how much of my blood and sweat are in this drink? Uh... figure of speech.
jokes.setup: Moe being passionate about his recipe
jokes.punchline: Clarification that it might literally contain bodily fluids
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor - making us question drink safety with Moe's clarification

jokes.text: Only an idiot would give away a million-dollar idea like that. Ohhh.
jokes.setup: Homer praising Moe for not selling recipe
jokes.punchline: Homer realizing he's the idiot who gave it away
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - inadvertently calling himself an idiot with realization

jokes.text: The inventor I admire is not a rich man or a famous man, or even a smart man. He's my father, Homer Simpson creator of... # Dun-da-da-dah # The Flaming Homer!
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliments leading to wrong name
jokes.explanation: Great setup-punchline with backhanded praise and dramatic musical sting

jokes.text: You mean the Flaming Moe and your dad didn't invent it, you wuss. Moe the bartender did. Everyone knows that.
jokes.punchline: Immediate public humiliation and correction
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Bart's public embarrassment over family pride

jokes.text: Henceforth, this date shall forever be known as Flaming Moe's Day. Sir, this is Veteran's Day. It can be two things.
jokes.punchline: Dismissing Veteran's Day conflict with absurd logic
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - bureaucratic dismissal of important holiday

jokes.text: Mom, instead of milk, can I have a virgin Moe?
jokes.punchline: Lisa wanting non-alcoholic version for breakfast
jokes.explanation: Good cultural satire - drink craze affecting even children's breakfast

jokes.text: Nah. Marge, I'm too upset to eat. I think I'll go to Moe's. D'oh! Oh!
jokes.punchline: Going to the source of his problem for comfort
jokes.explanation: Good character logic - Homer's self-defeating comfort-seeking behavior

jokes.text: Hello, St. Louis! That's Springfield, Steven. Oh, yeah. Right.
jokes.punchline: Getting the city wrong in typical rock star fashion
jokes.explanation: Standard rock star cliche - forgetting which city they're in

jokes.text: You're my kid's teacher. Single parent, are we? No. Let's pretend you are.
jokes.punchline: Ignoring rejection and creating fantasy scenario
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe creepiness - not taking no for an answer and fabricating scenario

jokes.text: It's hard to say. He may have come up with the recipe but I came up with the idea of charging 6.95.
jokes.punchline: Moe claiming credit for the obvious business practice of charging money
jokes.explanation: Great Moe logic - taking credit for basic business concept as innovation

jokes.text: I'm looking for a friend. Last name: Jazz. First name: Hugh. Hold on. I'll check. Huge ass? Somebody check the men's room for a huge ass. I'm Hugh Jazz. Telephone. This is Hugh Jazz. Uh... hi. Who's this? Bart Simpson. What can I do for you? This is a crank call that backfired and I'd like to bail out right now. Better luck next time.
jokes.punchline: Call backfires when real person with that name answers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expected prank call format - the impossible actually happens

jokes.text: Hmm, what a nice young man.
jokes.setup: Bart's honest confession about prank call
jokes.punchline: Genuine appreciation for Bart's honesty
jokes.explanation: Nice capper to prank call - unexpected wholesomeness from victim

jokes.text: You just lost yourself a customer! What? I couldn't hear you. I said, you just lost a customer! What? You just lost a customer! You'll have to speak up. You lost yourself a customer, Moe! I forced myself to what? You just lost a customer! We'll talk tomorrow. You just lost yourself a customer! Yeah, you can use it.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to make dramatic exit statement
jokes.punchline: Loud bar preventing effective dramatic gesture
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation comedy - Homer's dramatic exit completely undermined by volume

jokes.text: We're prepared to offer you $1 million. Sorry, Harv, but like I told you before it ain't for sale.
jokes.punchline: Moe turning down million dollars
jokes.explanation: Setup for bigger payoff - Moe's principled stand before revelation

jokes.text: According to the gas chromatograph the secret ingredient is... love?! Who's been screwing with this thing?
jokes.punchline: Absurd result suggesting sabotage
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - subverts scientific analysis with impossible result

jokes.text: I got hooked on this in the service.
jokes.setup: Customer buying cases of cough syrup
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of military-acquired addiction
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about addiction - treating serious issue casually

jokes.text: The Flaming Moe dates back to my forefathers who were bartenders to the czar.
jokes.setup: Moe being interviewed about his success
jokes.punchline: Fabricating elaborate family history for stolen recipe
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character - escalating lies to ridiculous proportions

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Mrs. Simpson but you can't copyright a drink. Ohh! This goes back to the '7 8 Frank Wallbanger case. How about that? I looked something up. These books don't just make the office look good. They're filled with useful legal tidbits.
jokes.punchline: Hutz's surprise at actually finding relevant information
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hutz characterization - amazed by his own competence

jokes.text: I'm the magical man from happy land in a gumdrop house on lollipop lane. Oh, by the way, I was being sarcastic. Well, duh!
jokes.punchline: Having to explain obvious sarcasm and Lisa's exasperated response
jokes.explanation: Great character dynamic - Homer's heavy-handed sarcasm and Lisa's intelligence

jokes.text: I sleep with a chick once it costs me half a million bananas.
jokes.setup: Moe deciding to share money with Homer
jokes.punchline: Crude characterization of his relationship with waitress
jokes.explanation: Typical Moe crudeness - reducing relationship to transactional terms

jokes.text: When Bart's done, can we Moe to the Moevies for the Moetinee? Of course. All work and Moe play makes Moe a Moe Moe.
jokes.setup: Family incorporating Moe's name into common phrases
jokes.punchline: Escalating substitution to the point of incomprehension
jokes.explanation: Good escalating wordplay showing the drink's cultural saturation

jokes.text: Moe, Moe, Moe... Bart, are you going to mow the lawn today? Okay, but you promised me Moe money. I Moe, I Moe. Moe, Moe, Moe... When Bart's done, can we Moe to the Moevies for the Moetinee? Of course. All work and Moe play makes Moe a Moe Moe. Moe Moe Moe Moe Moe? Moe Moe Moe. Moe Moe Moe Moe Moe. Moe Moe Moe Moe. Moe. Moe, Moe... Moe... Moe, Moe... Moe.... Moe, Moe...
jokes.punchline: Everything replaced with 'Moe' until language becomes meaningless
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - wordplay taken to absurd extreme until communication breaks down

jokes.text: Hey, where's Joey? [Joey ] Mrs. Krabappel I really need my drumsticks. Come and get 'em!
jokes.punchline: He's being seduced by teacher
jokes.explanation: Standard sex comedy - predictable teacher-student inappropriate relationship

jokes.text: The secret ingredient is... Homer, no! ...Cough syrup. Nothing but plain, ordinary, over-the-counter children's cough syrup.
jokes.punchline: Devastating revelation destroys Moe's empire
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff - Homer's petty revenge destroys million-dollar business with truth

jokes.text: Holy cow, you just fell on Aerosmith!
jokes.punchline: Homer's fall taking out celebrity guests
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy escalation - adding injury to insult

jokes.text: She left to pursue a movie career. Frankly, I think she was better off here.
jokes.punchline: Moe's delusional assessment of his establishment's appeal
jokes.explanation: Good character delusion - Moe thinking his dive bar beats Hollywood

jokes.text: Maybe some things are too good to be kept a secret.
jokes.setup: Homer apologizing for ruining Moe's business
jokes.punchline: Moe's philosophical acceptance of loss
jokes.explanation: Mild character growth moment - Moe showing unexpected wisdom

jokes.text: Compliments of the house-- One Flaming... Homer.
jokes.punchline: Finally giving drink its proper name
jokes.explanation: Nice resolution - Moe finally crediting Homer with proper name

tmdb_overview: Moe steals the drink recipe Homer invented, and the rechristened “Flaming Moe” turns his tavern into a hotspot. When a national franchise wants to buy the recipe, Homer wants revenge.
insight: Moe's identity crisis drives 68 jokes in 17 minutes—solid density masks middling impact.
